# weather

```go
import "github.com/lucasassuncao/gopaper/internal/weather"
```

Package weather provides current weather conditions \(via Open\-Meteo\) used by category variants that switch on sky condition or wind speed.

## Index

- [func IsValidSky\(name string\) bool](<#IsValidSky>)

  - [func \(s Snapshot\) Sky\(\) \(Sky, bool\)](<#Snapshot.Sky>)


<a name="IsValidSky"></a>
## func [IsValidSky](<https://github.com/lucasassuncao/gopaper/blob/main/internal/weather/sky.go#L63>)

```go
func IsValidSky(name string) bool
```

IsValidSky reports whether name is one of the known sky category names.

<a name="SkyNames"></a>
## func [SkyNames](<https://github.com/lucasassuncao/gopaper/blob/main/internal/weather/sky.go#L68>)

```go
func SkyNames() []string
```

SkyNames returns all known sky category names, sorted.

<a name="Config"></a>
## type [Config](<https://github.com/lucasassuncao/gopaper/blob/main/internal/weather/fetch.go#L30-L34>)

Config is the location and cache policy used to fetch a Snapshot.

```go
type Config struct {
    Latitude  float64
    Longitude float64
    CacheTTL  time.Duration
}
```

<a name="Sky"></a>
## type [Sky](<https://github.com/lucasassuncao/gopaper/blob/main/internal/weather/sky.go#L8>)

Sky is one of the weather categories a condition can match against.

```go
type Sky string
```

<a name="SkyClear"></a>

```go
const (
    SkyClear        Sky = "clear"
    SkyCloudy       Sky = "cloudy"
    SkyFog          Sky = "fog"
    SkyDrizzle      Sky = "drizzle"
    SkyRain         Sky = "rain"
    SkySnow         Sky = "snow"
    SkyThunderstorm Sky = "thunderstorm"
)
```

<a name="CodeToSky"></a>
### func [CodeToSky](<https://github.com/lucasassuncao/gopaper/blob/main/internal/weather/sky.go#L79>)

```go
func CodeToSky(code int) (sky Sky, ok bool)
```

CodeToSky maps a WMO weather code to a Sky category. ok is false for an unrecognized code.

<a name="Snapshot"></a>
## type [Snapshot](<https://github.com/lucasassuncao/gopaper/blob/main/internal/weather/fetch.go#L17-L21>)

Snapshot is a point\-in\-time weather reading.

```go
type Snapshot struct {
    Code        int     // WMO weather code
    WindSpeed   float64 // km/h
    Temperature float64 // Celsius
}
```

<a name="Fetch"></a>
### func [Fetch](<https://github.com/lucasassuncao/gopaper/blob/main/internal/weather/fetch.go#L50>)

```go
func Fetch(cfg Config, cachePath string) (Snapshot, error)
```

Fetch returns the current weather for cfg's location, using the cache at cachePath when it is fresh \(within cfg.CacheTTL\) and for the same location. On a live\-fetch failure it falls back to a stale cache entry for the same location if one exists; only when there is neither a fresh fetch nor any usable cache does it return an error.

<a name="Snapshot.Sky"></a>
### func \(Snapshot\) [Sky](<https://github.com/lucasassuncao/gopaper/blob/main/internal/weather/fetch.go#L25>)

```go
func (s Snapshot) Sky() (Sky, bool)
```

Sky maps the snapshot's code to a Sky category. ok is false for an unrecognized code.
